format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the '# <title>' H1; no leading YAML frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and parses with word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Exactly the six required '##' headings in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: Prose word count (excluding the ```mermaid``` block, L56-87) is ~1,300 words, inside the STANDARD 1200-1600 band. Reading-aloud (~1300/230 = 5.7 min) sits just under the 6-min read-aloud floor, but the governing word-count band passes comfortably; treated as in-band with a minor note. Meta reports 1240w / 7min, consistent.

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] (projector.tensorflow.org) cited multiple times (L24, L38, L101, L103). [2] and [3] also cited inline. All three inline markers resolve to References entries 1-3; no orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries. No fabricated markers.

forward_references.notes: No forward references. Cosine similarity (taught in 8.2 / earlier 6.9) is correctly NOT used. PCA/t-SNE/UMAP are named-and-deferred only ('different ways to squash the picture... How each method works is a more advanced topic you can explore later') — not taught, no internals. Embedding reused from prerequisite 6.7 without re-teaching, which is allowed.

faithfulness.notes: Sampled 10+ assertions (buttons analogy, domain definition, projector-draws-dots, dot=word, distance=relatedness, shadow analogy for projection, 2D-is-approximation, cluster/outlier definitions, plate-as-outlier, coach-in-between, two-cluster comparison, real-world sanity-check uses, do/don't habits). All trace to topic_corpus.md verbatim or paraphrased. Zero unsupported assertions.

beginner_readability.notes: Matches the fresher-zero-background baseline. Each non-everyday term (embedding, domain, projection, 2D, cluster, outlier) defined in plain language at first use; concrete-first (button piles, shadow on a wall) before abstraction; short sentences, active voice, 'you' address; no hedging phrases. No cold acronyms taught. No jargon dump.

formatting.notes: Scannable: bold-term/plain-meaning definitions, bulleted concept lists, a comparison table for cluster vs outlier, a numbered 7-step procedure in Worked Example, inline examples beside concepts. No paragraph exceeds ~4 sentences; no wall of text; no unlisted sequence.

scope.notes: On the headline ('comparing domain-specific word clusters in 2D'). No scope_creep: projection-method internals are mention-and-deferred as bare names, cosine similarity is absent, no LMS/assessment references. Nothing taught beyond the topic title at STANDARD depth.

diagram_embed.notes: A single inline ```mermaid``` flowchart sits at the 'Comparing two domains' point (L56-87) — a sensible location for the two-cluster/outlier/in-between idea — followed by an italic caption (L89). Consistent with meta diagram_embedded=true.

verdict: PASS. Reading 8.1 meets all reading-critic checks. Format clean (H1 first, no frontmatter, six canonical sections in order, sidecar valid). Prose ~1,300w lands inside the STANDARD 1200-1600 band; read-aloud ~5.7min is a hair under the 6-min floor but the word band governs. Primary resource [1] cited; [1][2][3] all resolve with no orphans. No forward references — cosine similarity (8.2) avoided and PCA/t-SNE/UMAP named-and-deferred without internals. Faithful to corpus across 10+ sampled assertions. Beginner tone and formatting strong (concrete-first analogies, bold-term definitions, numbered procedure, comparison table). On-scope, no scope_creep, no assessment prose. Mermaid diagram embedded inline at a sensible point with caption. No gold reading example present in .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ to bench against; judged against the written config standards.
